---
title: "American White Waterlily (Nymphaea odorata)"
slug: "nymphaea-odorata"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622091563/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "American White Waterlily"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Nymphaeales"
tax_family: "Nymphaeaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Nymphaeales", "Nymphaeaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622091563/medium.jpg" alt="American White Waterlily" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/brynnfricke">
brynnfricke</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341691843">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Nymphaeales
-   **Family:** Nymphaeaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=51119)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in still or slow-moving freshwater habitats.

## Natural history notes

An aquatic plant characterized by its large, round floating leaves and showy white flowers with numerous petals and a yellow center. In Florida, it is found in still or slow-moving freshwater habitats such as ponds, lakes, and marshes. It blooms during the warmer months, and its flowers open during the day, providing habitat and shade for aquatic organisms.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/51119-Nymphaea-odorata)
-   [Local profile](https://www.flawildflowers.org/flower-friday-nymphaea-odorata/)
